current_opportunities.name: Diller Teen Tikkun Olam Awards
current_opportunities.focus: Jewish youth leadership and social change initiatives
current_opportunities.notes: Up to 15 awards annually for Jewish teens leading change initiatives

current_opportunities.name: Diller Teen Fellows Program
current_opportunities.deadline: 2025-2026 cohort deadline passed; watch for next opening
current_opportunities.notes: Yearlong program with subsidies from foundation and Jewish Federation

how_to_apply.process: Unsolicited proposals NOT accepted. Foundation only funds through specific named programs
how_to_apply.requirements: Must apply through specific program websites (dillerteenawards.org or dillerteenfellows.org). Programs limited to Jewish youth
how_to_apply.notes: Direct grants to organizations outside of teen programs are not available

deadlines.type: Rolling for named programs; annual cycles for teen programs
deadlines.notes: Foundation does not accept unsolicited grant proposals

fit_assessment.notes: While Helen Diller Foundation supports health and youth programs, they explicitly do not accept unsolicited proposals and primarily focus on Jewish youth and UCSF cancer research. Friendship Circle SD, while serving special needs youth, is unlikely to qualify unless they have a Jewish education or teen component with connection to foundation priorities.
red_flags: Foundation does not accept unsolicited proposals
Grants primarily through named programs (Diller Teen Awards, Fellows)
Heavy focus on Jewish identity and Bay Area
Teen program focus limits relevance to broader special needs services
Multi-billion dollar grants to UCSF suggest focus on large institutional partnerships

current_opportunities.name: NYC Good Neighbor Committee
current_opportunities.notes: Limited to NYC-based organizations. Geographically restricted

current_opportunities.name: Disability Rights and Inclusion Grantmaking
current_opportunities.focus: Disability rights, economic justice for disabled people, policy advocacy, movement building
current_opportunities.notes: 15M commitment announced April 2025; at least 25% of all grantmaking inclusive of people with disabilities

how_to_apply.process: Does NOT accept unsolicited grant proposals by mail, email, or phone. Grants identified by foundation with own application processes
how_to_apply.requirements: Subscribe to newsletter to learn about opportunities; limited unsolicited submissions only through specific open programs. NYC Good Neighbor Committee applications typically require LOI and full proposal
how_to_apply.notes: Majority of grants are identified by foundation, not solicited from applicants

dates: Late Summer 2025 (NYC Good Neighbor Committee opens)
deadlines.notes: Most grants are by invitation only. Check website regularly for open opportunities

current_priorities: Building effective disability leaders and networks
Organizations led by people with disabilities

fit_assessment.notes: Ford Foundation's disability rights initiative aligns well with Friendship Circle's mission serving individuals with special needs. However, organization focuses on POLICY ADVOCACY and MOVEMENT BUILDING rather than direct service provision. Ford explicitly states they do not typically support direct service organizations. Geographic barrier: San Diego-based org competing nationally. Very competitive, limited opportunities available.
red_flags: Ford does not accept unsolicited proposals - must wait for specific solicitations
Foundation does not typically fund direct service provision (Friendship Circle's core activity)
Focus on policy advocacy, not service delivery
NYC Good Neighbor Committee is geographic-specific (not open to CA)
Highly competitive with 735M+ annual giving distributed to 3,718+ organizations
Fraud warning: Check legitimacy of any outreach claiming Ford funding

current_opportunities.name: General Grants Program
current_opportunities.notes: Approximately 8 grants awarded annually from California focus

how_to_apply.process: Online application through CyberGrants portal. Applications accepted year-round with June 30 deadline for annual funding cycle
how_to_apply.requirements: Must be 501(c)(3) organization operating in California; project must benefit underprivileged children; progress/final reports may be required
how_to_apply.notes: Automated email confirmation sent upon submission; decisions communicated by September

deadlines.notes: Applications accepted year-round but June 30 is preferred deadline for annual funding cycle

contact.program_officer: Wells Fargo Trust Philanthropic Services (fund administrator)

fit_assessment.notes: STRONG MATCH for Friendship Circle SD. Fund specifically supports underprivileged children in California with focus on health and human services. Friendship Circle serves children/youth with special needs in San Diego (California). Geographic match is perfect, funding focus (children, health/human services) aligns well. Grant size (5K-10K) is modest but accessible. Easy online application through CyberGrants.
red_flags: Small grant size (5-10K) limits potential award amount
Only ~8 grants given annually - competitive
Historical data from 2021-2023; verify current activity
Must demonstrate service to 'underprivileged children' (economic disadvantage emphasis)

current_opportunities.name: General Grants Program
current_opportunities.notes: Recent activity shows grants to Self-Help for the Elderly, Pinewood School, and Comfort Women Justice Coalition (2024)

how_to_apply.process: Contact foundation directly for application requirements. No formal application portal identified. Application form required
how_to_apply.requirements: No public guidelines available. Foundation recommends contacting directly for eligibility and application procedures
how_to_apply.notes: Small family foundation with minimal staff (no full-time employees). Direct contact preferred for inquiries

deadlines.notes: Foundation note states 'SEE ATTACHED' for deadlines/restrictions but attachments not publicly available

fit_assessment.notes: Limited fit with Friendship Circle. While Sik-Kee Au Foundation operates in California, their primary focus is seniors services and education (particularly Chinese heritage education). Recent grants show emphasis on Self-Help for the Elderly and educational institutions. Special needs youth services do not align well with stated priorities. Geographic focus in Cupertino/San Francisco (not San Diego area). Small foundation with modest grant size (5-15K).
red_flags: No public website or application guidelines
Primary focus on senior services (not children/youth)
Education focus appears heritage-specific (Chinese ancestry)
Geographic focus in SF Bay Area, not San Diego
Minimal staffing may indicate limited capacity for new grantees
Recent activity shows narrow beneficiary focus (self-help elderly, schools, justice coalition)
Foundation note 'SEE ATTACHED' for details suggests contact-dependent process

enrichments.foundation_name: A Part of the Whole A Family Foundation

current_opportunities.name: General Grants Program
current_opportunities.focus: Lakota People's Law Project, Indian child welfare, native rights, sacred lands protection
current_opportunities.notes: Recent 2023 grants: Gravity Water (72.5K), Yoga for All Movement (30K), Alas Ayudando Latinos A Sonar (10K)

how_to_apply.process: Contact foundation directly for application process. No public application portal identified
how_to_apply.requirements: No public guidelines published. Foundation contact recommends direct inquiry for eligibility and application requirements
how_to_apply.notes: Private family foundation registered in Delaware. Minimal public information available

deadlines.notes: No specific deadline information available publicly

current_priorities: Protection of sacred lands and waters
General operating support for aligned organizations

fit_assessment.notes: POOR FIT. While this foundation has larger grant capacity (36-109K), their mission focuses exclusively on Indigenous rights, Lakota communities, and Indian child welfare - NOT general special needs services. Friendship Circle SD serves diverse special needs individuals and is not Indigenous-specific. Geographic and programmatic mismatch is significant. Foundation appears to have narrow, specific grantee focus.
red_flags: Mission-specific to Lakota People's Law Project and Indian child welfare
No connection to general special needs services for non-Indigenous populations
Only 2-3 major grants annually (7 grants in 2023, with repeated grantee)
Limited repeat funding rate (12.5%) suggests selective grantee approach
Recent grants show focus on specific causes: Lakota law, water/environmental projects, yoga/wellness programs
No public application guidelines or website
Registered in Delaware with minimal public presence
Grant size suggests institutional/major organization focus, not small nonprofits

summary.top_recommendation: MARIAN & PINKUS HAPP FUND (EIN: 956025857) - Strong geographic match (California), funding focus (children health/human services), straightforward online application, reasonable grant size (5-10K), and perfect alignment with Friendship Circle's mission to serve underprivileged/special needs children
key_findings: Only 1 of 5 foundations (Happ Fund) shows strong programmatic and geographic alignment with Friendship Circle SD
Helen Diller Foundation and Ford Foundation do not accept unsolicited proposals - opportunities are limited and by invitation only
Sik-Kee Au and A Part of the Whole have narrow mission focus unrelated to special needs services
Best opportunity: Apply to Happ Fund by June 30, 2025 deadline via CyberGrants portal
Ford Foundation's disability rights initiative could be relevant IF Friendship Circle can reposition toward advocacy/movement building (unlikely given service focus)
Note: Opportunities file ranked these foundations as high-match, but enrichment research reveals limited actual fit for direct service organization
